architecture_decisions.decision: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS) for tenant data isolation
architecture_decisions.rationale: Provides database-level enforcement of tenant isolation, preventing cross-tenant data leakage even if application logic fails. PostgreSQL 16 has mature RLS support with performance optimizations.

architecture_decisions.decision: Tenant context propagation via request context
architecture_decisions.rationale: Eliminates need to pass organization_id through every function call. Middleware extracts tenant from JWT claims and stores in context, automatically used by repository layers.
Shared tenant logic (context extraction, middleware, repository helpers) across all microservices ensures consistency and reduces code duplication.

architecture_decisions.decision: Resource quota enforcement at service layer with database tracking
architecture_decisions.rationale: Allows real-time quota checking before resource allocation while maintaining audit trail. Database-level constraints prevent quota overflow.
Meets compliance requirements (US-009) and enables per-tenant auditing without cross-tenant data exposure.
Separate queue keys per tenant (e.g., jobs:{tenant_id}) ensures background jobs automatically respect tenant boundaries (US-006).

database_migrations.sql:
CREATE TABLE resource_usage (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    resource_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    current_value BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    period_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    period_end TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(organization_id, resource_type, period_start)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_resource_usage_org_period ON resource_usage(organization_id, period_start);

CREATE TABLE tenant_audit_logs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    resource_type VARCHAR(50),
    resource_id UUID,
    old_values JSONB,
    new_values JSONB,
    ip_address INET,
    user_agent TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_logs_org_id ON tenant_audit_logs(organization_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_logs_user_id ON tenant_audit_logs(user_id, created_at DESC);

security_notes: All database queries must use RLS policies as defense-in-depth
JWT tokens must include organization_id claim signed by server
Tenant context must be validated on every service boundary
Quota checks must be atomic to prevent race conditions
Audit logs must be written before any data modification
Organization owners can only be transferred, not removed
API responses must not leak existence of other organizations
Rate limiting must be applied per-organization
Admin bypass of RLS must require explicit privileges
S3 documents must be prefixed with organization_id for isolation
Background jobs must inherit tenant context from job queue message
WebSocket connections must validate tenant on subscription
Audit logs for cross-admin actions must be immutable
docker_changes: Add postgres-init script to set up RLS policies
Update health checks to include tenant context validation
Add database migration for existing data to default organization

market_driven_features.description: Track print costs by department/organization with detailed reporting, enabling chargeback to business units.

market_driven_features.feature: Secure print release with per-organization queues
market_driven_features.description: Users release print jobs at any device within their organization, with authentication required.
Organizations can have multiple sites/branches with hierarchical management and centralized reporting.
Mobile users can print to their organization's printers from anywhere, respecting tenant boundaries.
Organization-configurable dynamic watermarks on documents including user name, timestamp, and organization.
Per-organization configurable approval workflows for large print jobs or sensitive documents.
Organization can configure rules to detect and flag sensitive content (SSN, confidential) in print jobs.
Detailed audit trail per organization including user actions, document access, and admin changes.
Printers automatically assigned to organization based on network/location with manual override.
Organizations can configure print optimization settings (quality vs speed defaults).
Organizations define custom print routing rules based on document type, user, or printer capabilities.

market_driven_features.feature: Document security with organization encryption keys
market_driven_features.description: Each organization can have optional encryption keys for stored documents with key rotation support.
